test_cases.title: Bulk reassign selected tasks to a different user successfully
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least two tasks exist in the task list
At least one other user exists in the system to reassign tasks to
steps: Navigate to the task list view
Select two or more tasks using their individual checkboxes
Invoke the bulk 'Reassign' operation from the bulk actions menu/toolbar
Select a target user from the reassignment options
Confirm the reassignment action
test_cases.expected_result: All selected tasks are reassigned to the chosen user simultaneously, and the task list reflects the updated assignee for every selected task without any partial updates.

test_cases.title: Bulk delete displays confirmation dialog with correct task count
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least three tasks exist in the task list
steps: Navigate to the task list view
Select exactly three tasks using their individual checkboxes
Invoke the bulk 'Delete' operation from the bulk actions menu/toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation dialog is displayed showing the count of tasks to be deleted as exactly 3.

test_cases.title: Bulk delete proceeds and removes all selected tasks after confirmation
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least two tasks exist in the task list
The bulk delete confirmation dialog is displayed with the correct task count
steps: Navigate to the task list view
Select two tasks using their individual checkboxes
Invoke the bulk 'Delete' operation
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
test_cases.expected_result: All selected tasks are deleted atomically and no longer appear in the task list; unselected tasks remain unaffected.

test_cases.title: Bulk delete cancelled from confirmation dialog leaves tasks intact
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least two tasks exist in the task list
steps: Navigate to the task list view
Select two tasks using their individual checkboxes
Invoke the bulk 'Delete' operation
Click 'Cancel' or dismiss the confirmation dialog without confirming
test_cases.expected_result: No tasks are deleted; all previously selected tasks remain in the task list and their selection state is preserved or reset without data loss.

test_cases.title: Bulk status change applied atomically to all selected tasks
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least two tasks with different current statuses exist in the task list
Multiple valid task statuses are available in the system
steps: Navigate to the task list view
Select two or more tasks using their individual checkboxes
Invoke the bulk 'Change Status' operation from the bulk actions menu/toolbar
Select a target status from the available options
Confirm the status change
test_cases.expected_result: All selected tasks have their status updated to the chosen status simultaneously; no task is partially updated or left with its original status.

test_cases.title: Unauthorized user cannot perform bulk operations on tasks
preconditions: A user account exists that does not have permission to perform bulk operations (e.g., read-only role)
At least two tasks exist in the task list visible to this user
steps: Authenticate as the unauthorized/read-only user
Navigate to the task list view
Attempt to select tasks using checkboxes
Attempt to invoke a bulk operation (reassign, change status, or delete)
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies the bulk operation and does not modify any tasks; an appropriate error or restriction message is displayed or the bulk action controls are not available to this user.

assumptions: Atomic update means either all selected tasks are updated successfully or none are (all-or-nothing transaction); a partial failure rolls back all changes.

A user must select at least one task before bulk action controls become actionable; the requirement does not specify behavior for zero-task selection, so it is assumed the bulk actions are disabled or not accessible.
There is a dedicated bulk actions interface (e.g., toolbar or context menu) that becomes available when one or more checkboxes are selected.
The system has a role or permission model that controls access to bulk operations; at least one role exists that lacks this permission.
Task statuses available for bulk change are the same statuses available for individual task updates.
The confirmation dialog for bulk delete contains at minimum a confirm and a cancel action.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) what happens when a bulk operation partially fails on the server side beyond stating atomicity — error messaging and rollback behavior are not described; (2) whether there is a maximum limit on the number of tasks that can be selected for a bulk operation; (3) the exact UI mechanism for triggering bulk operations (toolbar, right-click menu, floating button, etc.); (4) whether selecting all tasks via a 'select all' checkbox is required — no test case covers this as it is not mentioned; (5) whether bulk operations span multiple pages of results or only the currently visible page; (6) the behavior when a selected task is modified or deleted by another user between selection and confirmation. These gaps may require additional test cases once the requirement is clarified.
